claim: Task quality has a 5x impact on training uplift: low-quality tasks improved the base model by 1%, high-quality tasks improved it by 6%, on the same model with same compute budget
actionable: Prioritize task quality in RL training datasets—invest in task verification harnesses to reject low-quality tasks before training.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: High-quality agentic tasks average twice as many tool calls, have lower pass rates (harder), and require more output tokens—demonstrating genuine complexity vs. noise
actionable: Use tool call count, pass rate, and output token requirements as proxies for task quality when evaluating RL training datasets.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Accepted tasks produce cleaner failure modes (logic errors, incomplete reasoning); rejected tasks fail due to mismatches between task spec and tests or missing context—ambiguous specs create noise, not difficulty
actionable: Design task specs and tests to be unambiguous; task difficulty should come from genuine problem complexity, not unclear requirements.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Task quality criteria: achievable (non-trivial), functionally correct logic, reliable environment, and reproducibility via containerization—these four criteria correlate with model performance gains
actionable: Build task acceptance harnesses that verify all four criteria before including tasks in RL training datasets.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Environmental failures (task can't be completed by any model) and specification mismatches are noise—they don't provide meaningful training signal and inflate failure rates without improving models
actionable: Categorize failures into meaningful (model reasoning gaps) and noise (environment/spec issues); exclude noise-based failures from training datasets.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Implicit task dependencies that aren't provided as context cause model failures even though the model has correct reasoning—clear spec means all context is explicit
actionable: When writing task specifications, ensure all dependencies and requirements are explicitly stated; don't assume model will infer implicit context.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Human-expert-in-the-loop is critical for task quality; leverage LLM judges trained on expert rubrics and verified inter-rater agreement (human-human and LLM-human) to scale quality assessment
actionable: Use human experts to define detailed rubrics, have LLM judges apply those rubrics, and measure inter-rater agreement to scale quality assessment.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel

claim: Treat evals like tests (versioned in code, run in CI, not dashboards)—evals compound over time, preventing regression and building a regression suite automatically
actionable: Version eval tasks alongside code; every bug fix and training run should generate new eval tasks that become permanent regression tests.
source_title: Task Fidelity Scaling Laws — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
